claim: The handoff test used only the previous run's idea_ledger.csv as provider input.
evidence_quote_or_paraphrase: The spec defines the scope as using only the previous run's idea_ledger.csv as provider input.

claim: The input ledger contains six idea rows with IDs, premise, evidence rows, status, next_test, risk, and critic verdict.
evidence_quote_or_paraphrase: The input CSV includes I1-I6 and columns idea_id,title,premise,evidence_rows,status,next_test,main_risk,critic_verdict.
notes: Shows ledger can be parsed as structured handoff input.

claim: Agy returned a usable CSV preserving all six idea IDs and most fields.
evidence_quote_or_paraphrase: Agy returned CSV rows for I1-I6 and changed I1 status from candidate to validated and critic_verdict from KEEP to ACCEPT.
conflicts: Agy promoted I1 without new evidence beyond the handoff itself.

claim: Agy over-promoted I1 by marking it validated from the handoff alone.
evidence_quote_or_paraphrase: Agy changed I1 status to validated and next_test to monitor schema against 07_ideas.md.
conflicts: The test did not provide new external evidence or a completed downstream implementation.

claim: Claude preserved the ledger and refused to update without new evidence.
evidence_quote_or_paraphrase: Claude said it received only the ledger snapshot with no new test results, evidence rows, or run data to justify updates, then returned the ledger as-is.

claim: Provider divergence indicates the ledger needs explicit update policy or stricter status semantics.
evidence_quote_or_paraphrase: Agy treated handoff completion as validation; Claude treated absence of new evidence as reason not to update. Normalized ledger keeps I1 as candidate_validated_once rather than fully validated.
